headline: Alex Eala climbs to career-high no. 162 in WTA rankings  GMA News Online
text:
Filipina tennis ace Alex Eala climbed to a new career-high in the latest Women's Tennis Association rankings released on May 6.
Eala is currently at no. 162 in her singles ranking, eight spots higher than her previous rank at no. 170 last April 22.
The rankings came after she competed in the Mutua Madrid Open where she pulled off an upset against WTA no. 41 Lesia Tsurenko of Ukraine in the tournament's first round.
She then fell short against no. 30 Sorana Cirstea in the Round of 64.
In doubles, Eala also rose to career-high no. 239 after ranking at no. 243 weeks ago.
—JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: Marcos urges Zamboanga, neighboring towns to prepare for rainy season  GMA News Online
text:
President Ferdinand ''Bongbong'' Marcos Jr. on Thursday called on the local government of Zamboanga and neighboring areas to prepare for the rainy season, saying that the country is experiencing extreme weather due to climate change. 
Marcos led the distribution of assistance to farmers, fisherfolk, and families in a program at the Universidad de Zamboanga Summit Center in Barangay Tetuan, Zamboanga City. 
''Ang buong mundo po ay [nahaharap] sa matinding pagsubok dulot sa tinatawag na climate change, pagbabago po ng panahon. Extreme weather ang nararanasan natin. Matinding init ngayon at sa mga susunod na buwan naman ay asahan natin ay ang matindi naman na pag-ulan,'' Marcos said in his speech. 
(The entire world is facing the challenge brought by climate change. We are experiencing extreme weather. We are encountering too much heat and in the next months, we will experience strong rains.) ''Kaya naman po, nananawagan ako sa lokal na pamahalaan ng Zamboanga at sa mga karatig-bayan [nito sa] buong rehiyon. Maging handa po kayo sa lahat ng oras,'' he added.
(Due to that, I am calling on the local government of Zamboanga and the neighboring towns to prepare.) 
Marcos ordered the creation of programs that would help their constituents. 
''Alamin natin ang pangangailangan ng ating mga nasasakupan at gumawa tayo ng mga programa na tiyak na makakatulong sa ating mga kababayan,'' Marcos said. 
(Identify the needs of our constituents and create programs that will help our kababayans.) 
Marcos distributed financial aid amounting to P10,000 for beneficiaries from Region 9. 
The President also extended monetary support totaling around P58 million to the City of Zamboanga and the provinces of Zamboanga del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte and Zamboanga Sibugay.—LDF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Cop who drove PNP van in EDSA busway relieved for violation  GMA News Online
text:
The policeman assigned to drive a Philippine National Police van who was unauthorized to use the EDSA busway has been relieved, according to PNP spokesperson Police Colonel Jean Fajardo on Thursday.In a press briefing, Fajardo said the driver of the van was designated at the Police Region Office 10 (PRO 10).“Nakausap ko mismo yung Regional Director ng PRO 10, ni-relieved na po niya itong authorized driver po at haharap po siya administrative case for failure to follow specific instruction na bawal na po dumaan kahit na anong marked vehicles dyan sa EDSA busway,” Fajardo said.(I personally spoke with the PRO 10 Regional Director, he has relieved this authorized driver and he will face an administrative case for failure to follow specific instructions that no marked vehicles are allowed to pass through the EDSA busway.)The PNP vehicle reportedly evaded apprehension by traffic enforcers.According to Fajardo, the PNP is still checking if the authorized driver was the one driving the PNP van and if more policemen were inside the vehicle during the incident.Fajardo said the concerned cop should comply with the show cause order issued by the Department of Transportation (DOTr) in connection with the incident.
Only the following vehicles are authorized to use the busway:

headline: Tickets to 'Virgin Labfest 19 Pintog' in June now up for grabs  GMA News Online
text:
Virgin Labfest, the annual festival of untried, untested, and unstaged one-act plays, returns this June with its 19th edition "Pintog."
The event will run from June 12-30 at Tanghalang Ignacio Gimenez (CCP Black Box Theater) at the Cultural Center of the Philippines in Malate, Manila.
The CCP announced Wednesday on Facebook that tickets to the fest are now up for grabs via Ticketworld. Regular and premium tickets are each priced at P600 and P800, respectively.
Meanwhile, regular (P2,400) and premium (P3,200) festival passes are available for purchase at the CCP Box Office from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m., Tuesday to Friday.
"VLF 19: Pintog," which is jointly produced by the CCP, Tanghalang Pilipino Foundation Inc., and The Writer's Bloc, will present 12 new scripts that confront the complexities of the human experience.
These include Hans Pieter Arao's "Vengeance of the Gods," Lino Balmes' "Ningas," Rick Patriarca's "Love on the Brain," Joshua Lim So's "Pagkapit sa Hangin," Ara Jenika Vinzon's "Lipistick at Pulbura," Chesie Galvez-Cariño's "The Foxtrot," Elise Santos' "Sa Babaeng Lahat," Dustin Celestino's "Ang Munting Liwanag sa Madilim na Sulok ng Serbeserya sa Maynila," Neil Azcuna's "Si Hesus na'a sa U.S.," Heryln Alegre's "Sentenaryo," Dip Mariposque's "The Divine Family," and Jhudiel Clare Sosa's "Identite."
Three plays from last year's "VLF 18: Hitik" will also be revisited in the June event, namely Andrew Estacio's "Awit ng Dalagang Marmol," Dingdong Novenario's "Dominador Gonzales: National Artist," and Zheg Arban's "Room 209."
Four script submissions to "VLF 19" will also have staged readings: Christa De La Cruz's "Huling Salita," Dia Sales' "Pabili ng Christmas Tree," Atria Pacana's "Shiver," and Floyd Scott Tioganco's "Patayin ang mga Surot."
Visit the CCP's website for more information on the plays in this year's "VLF 19."
— Cody Cepeda/LA, GMA Integrated News

headline: Dingdong Dantes reveals which of the TV characters he's portrayed is most like him in real life  GMA News Online
text:
With a career spanning over 20 years, Dingdong Dantes has played various characters in the numerous movies and TV series he's done.  
In the sidelines of his renewal of contract with GMA Network, the Kapuso Primetime King said it is the character of Iñaki Torres from the '90s youth-oriented show “T.G.I.S” that resonates with him the most. 
“Only because parang basic lang naman siya na teenager na makulit. Ganon din naman dati na walang direksyon. Gusto lang magpa-cute palagi sa mga chicks. Yung mga ganong bagay. ‘Yon lang naman yon,” Dingdong told GMA News Online during the sidelines of his contract renewal with the Kapuso Network on Thursday. 
[Only because he was a basic teenager. I was like that before, seemingly without direction. I just wanted to charm girls, stuff like that.]
“But everything else, all other roles, may konting trait na medyo ako. So kung hindi man entirely may konting trait ng character na ito na medyo similar sa akin,” he added.
Dingdong shot to popularity in the '90s for portraying the charmismatic and confident 'Iñaki Torres, the younger cousin of Bobby Andrews' Joaquin "Wacks" Torres III, in “T.G.I.S."
When the Kapuso actor renewed his ties with GMA Network on Thursday, he couldn't help but tear up at the congratulatory messages he received from GMA executives.
"Sa aking mga boss, maraming maraming salamat," Dingdong said. "Thank you po for those kind words, I truly appreciate them."
He also expressed appreciation for the relationships he established along the way, particularly with the production crews, the staff on set, and his co-actors, saying that they made his every chapter in GMA memorable.
Dingdong's first show in GMA, since then, he's starred in several Kapuso series such as “Encantadia,” “Alyas Robinhood,” and “Dyesebel” to name a few.
He also top-billed some award-winning movies, most recently “Rewind.”
He hosts "Amazing Earth" and "Family Feud." 
— LA, GMA Integrated News

headline: Lady Gaga's 'The Chromatica Ball' film to premiere this May on HBO  GMA News Online
text:
Little Monsters, this one's for you!
"The Chromatica Ball," Lady Gaga's 2022 concert held in the Dodger Stadium in Los Angeles, is getting a film version and is set to premiere on HBO on May 26.
In the Philippines, "The Chromatica Ball" film will be streamed on HBO GO on May 25.  
The announcement came from Lady Gaga's social media pages, where she posted a video teaser of the upcoming concert film.
"I'm so excited that we can finally share 'The Chromatica Ball' film with the world," the "Born This Way" hitmaker wrote.
"This film chronicles a time of immense creativity… the fashion, the dance, the music," she added.
According to Gaga, revisiting the tour through the concert film "leaves me speechless" because of the support that she got during the sold-out concerts.
"I love you monsters more than I can say," she said.  "Here's the truth—no matter where life or my career has taken me, my time with you always is a path back to a very powerful part of myself."
Gaga ended her post by saying that she edited the film carefully to honor all her fans. 
"I hope you will feel seen when you watch this film," she said.
Lady Gaga released the album "Chromatica" in May 2020. 
In January, the pop star teased fans about a music comeback nearly four years since her last album.
A post shared by Lady Gaga (@ladygaga)
—Hermes Joy Tunac/MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: PH economy grows by 5.7% in Q1 2024  GMA News Online
text:
Philippine economic growth saw an acceleration in the first quarter of the year from the three months prior but slowed from the same period in the past year, government data released on Thursday showed.
Data from the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) showed that the gross domestic product (GDP) grew by 5.7% in the first quarter of the year, which compares with the downward-revised 5.5% in the fourth quarter of 2023 and the 6.4% in January to March of 2023.
The latest reading is lower than the target range of 6.0% to 7.0% set by the inter-agency Development Budget Coordination Committee (DBCC), which was cut short from the prior goal of 6.5% to 7.5% to reflect factors such as global demand, trade growth, oil prices, and inflation trends.
In a briefing, National Statistician Claire Dennis Mapa said the main contributors to growth were financial and insurance activities at 10.0%, wholesale and retail trade and the repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles at 6.4%, and manufacturing at 4.5%.
All major economic sectors posted annual growths — agriculture, forestry, and fishing (AFF) with 0.4%, industry with 0.4%, and services with 6.9%.
Gross national income (GNI) had an annual growth of 9.7%, while the net primary income (NPI) from the rest of the world expanded by 57.0%.
On the demand side, household final consumption expenditure (HFCE) grew by 4.6%, which was the slowest since the third quarter of 2010 amid the country’s elevated inflation and the ongoing El Niño.
"It's a combination of factors of both domestic and external. On the domestic front, it's obviously the elevated inflation and the high interest rates," National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Secretary Arsenio Balisacan said.
"Note that the high interest rates, particularly the policy rates that were implemented by the (Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas) were meant to address certain issues," he added.
The central bank in February kept policy rates unchanged at 16-year highs — the target reverse repurchase (RRP) at 6.5%, the overnight deposit rate at 6.0%, and the overnight lending facility rate at 7.0%. These were also maintained in April, with the next policy meeting scheduled on May 16.
Inflation, meanwhile, averaged 3.3% in the first quarter. It has since clocked in at 3.8% in April, marking the fastest in four months.
Balisacan said the elevated heat levels also affected production, especially construction activities and the services sector.
“Construction slowed down, no doubt affected by prolonged periods of extreme heat. Household spending also slowed due to elevated prices of major food items and the heat wave,” Balisacan said.
“People don’t want to go out. It’s so hot, I think that also affected the construction,” he added, with construction growth slowing to 6.8% from 10.1% in the previous quarter, and the 14.7% expansion in the first quarter of 2023.
Government spending grew by 1.7% during the quarter, faster than the 1.0% contraction in the fourth quarter of 2023, but slower than the 6.2%.
Balisacan said, “Government spending also slowed down, primarily due to the sliding of a large amount of expenditure to April this year, whereas the government made such spending in March last year.”
Moving forward, Balisacan said the country is still on track to meet its target this year given the efforts put in place by the government to address the El Niño and the high inflation.
"I think that [is] unless we see a reversal in the gains we are making in tempering the inflation, particularly food and energy, I don't see any reason why the second quarter should be any weaker than the first quarter," he said.
With the heat levels remaining elevated through the second quarter, Balisacan said the government is taking climate change seriously as it is now tapping stakeholders to join efforts to adapt especially in vulnerable areas such as agriculture.
"I don't see any more serious efforts now in dealing with climate change than what we are putting today because we see it now, experience it now on almost a daily basis, so we are putting a lot of attention there," he said.  — KBK/VDV, GMA Integrated News
